# Eval Report

## Summary

The five-layer upgrade is implemented and validated with automated tests plus a TDWH HTML sampling smoke check. PDF support and three-screen lazy loading have also been added. No P0/P1 gate remains failing.

## Hard Gates

| Gate | Command or check | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| lint | `npm run lint` | Pass | Checked `extension/manifest.json` and 18 extension files. |
| typecheck | N/A | N/A | Project is plain JS with Node tests. |
| test | `npm test` | Pass | 75 tests, 0 failures. |
| build | N/A | N/A | Unpacked Chrome MV3 extension has no build step. |
| UI audit | Code review | Pass | Progress UI is a small fixed status pill; no intrusive overlay. |
| local deploy | Unpacked extension path | Pass | Load `tools/toktra/extension` in Chrome. |

## Score

| Dimension | Weight | Score | Notes |

| product value | 15 | 14 | Covers realtime translation efficiency, visibility, and rule control. |
| functional completeness | 15 | 14 | P0/P1 criteria pass; no Chrome live-page automation in this run. |

| iteration evidence and summary | 5 | 4 | Longtask docs updated; no daemon/orchestrator run beyond init. |

## Failures

- Initial red tests failed as expected for missing `translateSegments`, namespaced cache, segment metadata, scheduler, rules, and progress rendering.
- A progress removal test expectation was corrected because injected style is also removable toktra UI.

## Repairs

- Scheduler comparator was adjusted after TDWH sampling showed heading priority could override DOM order in layout-less environments. Visual/DOM order now wins; priority is a final tie-breaker.

## Score Delta

Final weighted score: 92/100.

## Remaining Risk

- Content-script behavior is indirectly tested through core/background contracts; a Chrome live-page smoke run is still useful before packaging.
- Provider layer currently has only OpenAI-compatible implementation, but the contract is ready for additional providers.
- PDF support is text-layer based. It preserves reading order and bilingual rendering, but it is not a pixel-perfect reproduction of the original PDF layout.
